library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.2.0     ✔ readr     2.2.0
## ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
## ✔ ggplot2   4.0.2     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.5     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
## ✔ purrr     1.2.1     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(forecast)
library(tseries)
## Registered S3 method overwritten by 'quantmod':
##   method            from
##   as.zoo.data.frame zoo
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(scales)
## 
## Attaching package: 'scales'
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:purrr':
## 
##     discard
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:readr':
## 
##     col_factor
library(nortest)
INPUT_FILE  <- "../../data/processed/merged_features_weekly.csv"
OUTPUT_DIR  <- "outputs"
FREQ        <- 52
ALPHA       <- 0.05
df <- read_csv(INPUT_FILE, show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(date = as.Date(date)) %>%
  arrange(date)

target_commodities <- c(
  "Beetroot",
  "bulb onion yellow",
  "cabbage",
  "carrot",
  "cucumber",
  "curly kale",
  "lettuce",
  "tomato round"
)

df <- df %>%
  filter(commodity %in% target_commodities)

commodities <- unique(df$commodity)
cat("Filtered commodities:", paste(commodities, collapse = ", "), "\n")
## Filtered commodities: Beetroot
calc_rmse  <- function(actual, pred) sqrt(mean((actual - pred)^2, na.rm = TRUE))
calc_mae   <- function(actual, pred) mean(abs(actual - pred), na.rm = TRUE)
calc_mape  <- function(actual, pred) mean(abs((actual - pred) / actual) * 100, na.rm = TRUE)
calc_smape <- function(actual, pred) {
  mean(200 * abs(actual - pred) / (abs(actual) + abs(pred)), na.rm = TRUE)
}
calc_mase  <- function(actual, pred, train) {
  naive_mae <- mean(abs(diff(train)), na.rm = TRUE)
  mean(abs(actual - pred), na.rm = TRUE) / naive_mae
}

compile_metrics <- function(actual, pred, train, model_obj, label) {
  tibble(
    model = label,
    RMSE  = round(calc_rmse(actual, pred),        4),
    MAE   = round(calc_mae(actual, pred),         4),
    MASE  = round(calc_mase(actual, pred, train), 4),
    sMAPE = round(calc_smape(actual, pred),       4),
    MAPE  = round(calc_mape(actual, pred),        4),
    AIC   = round(AIC(model_obj),                 2),
    BIC   = round(BIC(model_obj),                 2)
  )
}

residual_diagnostics <- function(resid_vec, model_obj, label) {
  resid_clean <- resid_vec[!is.na(resid_vec)]
  lb10 <- Box.test(resid_clean, lag = 10, type = "Ljung-Box")
  lb20 <- Box.test(resid_clean, lag = 20, type = "Ljung-Box")
  if (length(resid_clean) <= 5000) {
    norm_test <- shapiro.test(resid_clean); norm_name <- "Shapiro-Wilk"
  } else {
    norm_test <- nortest::ad.test(resid_clean); norm_name <- "Anderson-Darling"
  }
  tibble(
    model             = label,
    resid_mean        = round(mean(resid_clean),     6),
    resid_sd          = round(sd(resid_clean),       4),
    ljung_box_lag10_p = round(lb10$p.value,          4),
    ljung_box_lag20_p = round(lb20$p.value,          4),
    normality_test    = norm_name,
    normality_p       = round(norm_test$p.value,     4),
    autocorr_ok       = lb10$p.value > ALPHA,
    normality_ok      = norm_test$p.value > ALPHA
  )
}

plot_residuals <- function(resid_vec, fitted_vec, label) {
  resid_clean <- resid_vec[!is.na(resid_vec)]
  time_idx    <- seq_along(resid_vec)
  ci_bound    <- qnorm(0.975) / sqrt(length(resid_clean))

  p1 <- ggplot(data.frame(t = time_idx, r = resid_vec), aes(t, r)) +
    geom_line(color = "#2c3e50", linewidth = 0.45) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
    labs(title = "Residuals over time", x = "Week index", y = "Residual") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

  acf_data <- acf(resid_clean, plot = FALSE, lag.max = 52)
  acf_df   <- data.frame(lag = acf_data$lag[-1], acf = acf_data$acf[-1])
  p2 <- ggplot(acf_df, aes(lag, acf)) +
    geom_segment(aes(xend = lag, yend = 0), color = "#2980b9") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = c(-ci_bound, ci_bound), linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "black") +
    labs(title = "ACF of residuals", x = "Lag (weeks)", y = "ACF") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

  pacf_data <- pacf(resid_clean, plot = FALSE, lag.max = 52)
  pacf_df   <- data.frame(lag = pacf_data$lag, pacf = pacf_data$acf)
  p3 <- ggplot(pacf_df, aes(lag, pacf)) +
    geom_segment(aes(xend = lag, yend = 0), color = "#8e44ad") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = c(-ci_bound, ci_bound), linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "black") +
    labs(title = "PACF of residuals", x = "Lag (weeks)", y = "PACF") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

  p4 <- ggplot(data.frame(r = resid_clean), aes(r)) +
    geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), bins = 40,
                   fill = "#3498db", color = "white", alpha = 0.7) +
    stat_function(fun = dnorm,
                  args = list(mean = mean(resid_clean), sd = sd(resid_clean)),
                  color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
    labs(title = "Residual distribution", x = "Residual", y = "Density") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

  qq    <- qqnorm(resid_clean, plot.it = FALSE)
  qq_df <- data.frame(theoretical = qq$x, sample = qq$y)
  p5 <- ggplot(qq_df, aes(theoretical, sample)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.4, color = "#2c3e50", size = 0.8) +
    geom_abline(color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
    labs(title = "Q-Q plot", x = "Theoretical quantiles", y = "Sample quantiles") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

  p6 <- ggplot(data.frame(f = fitted_vec, r = resid_vec), aes(f, r)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.35, size = 0.9, color = "#16a085") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_smooth(method = "loess", formula = y ~ x, se = FALSE,
                color = "#e67e22", linewidth = 0.8) +
    labs(title = "Residuals vs Fitted", x = "Fitted values", y = "Residual") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

  combined <- (p1 | p4) / (p2 | p3) / (p5 | p6) +
    plot_annotation(
      title    = paste0("Residual Diagnostics — ", label),
      subtitle = "Row 1: Time series & distribution | Row 2: ACF & PACF | Row 3: Q-Q & Residuals vs Fitted",
      theme    = theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14))
    )
  print(combined)
}

plot_forecast <- function(train_dates, train_vals,
                          test_dates,  test_vals,
                          fc_list, label) {
  train_df <- data.frame(date = train_dates, value = train_vals) %>%
    filter(date >= max(date) - 365 * 2)
  test_df  <- data.frame(date = test_dates, value = test_vals)

  model_colors <- c(
    "Train"          = "#bdc3c7",
    "Actual"         = "#2c3e50",
    "Additive"       = "#e74c3c",
    "Multiplicative" = "#2980b9",
    "ETS (auto)"     = "#27ae60"
  )

  p <- ggplot() +
    geom_line(data = train_df, aes(x = date, y = value, color = "Train"), linewidth = 0.6) +
    geom_line(data = test_df,  aes(x = date, y = value, color = "Actual"), linewidth = 1)

  ribbon_alpha <- c(0.12, 0.10, 0.09)
  for (i in seq_along(fc_list)) {
    fc_name <- names(fc_list)[i]
    fc_obj  <- fc_list[[i]]
    pred_df <- data.frame(
      date  = test_dates,
      pred  = as.numeric(fc_obj$mean),
      lo95  = as.numeric(fc_obj$lower[, 2]),
      hi95  = as.numeric(fc_obj$upper[, 2]),
      model = fc_name
    )
    p <- p +
      geom_ribbon(data = pred_df, aes(x = date, ymin = lo95, ymax = hi95),
                  fill = model_colors[fc_name], alpha = ribbon_alpha[i]) +
      geom_line(data = pred_df, aes(x = date, y = pred, color = model),
                linewidth = 0.85, linetype = "dashed")
  }

  p <- p +
    scale_color_manual(values = model_colors, name = NULL) +
    labs(
      title    = paste0("Holt-Winters Forecast — ", label),
      subtitle = "Dashed = forecast | Shaded = 95% prediction interval",
      x = NULL, y = "Price"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"), legend.position = "bottom")

  print(p)
}

plot_components <- function(model_obj, label) {
  comp <- data.frame(
    index    = seq_len(nrow(model_obj$states)),
    level    = model_obj$states[, "l"],
    trend    = if ("b"  %in% colnames(model_obj$states)) model_obj$states[, "b"]  else NA,
    seasonal = if ("s1" %in% colnames(model_obj$states)) model_obj$states[, "s1"] else NA
  )

  plots <- list(
    ggplot(comp, aes(index, level)) +
      geom_line(color = "#2c3e50", linewidth = 0.7) +
      labs(title = "Level component", x = "Week index", y = "Level") +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 11)
  )

  if (!all(is.na(comp$trend))) {
    plots <- c(plots, list(
      ggplot(comp, aes(index, trend)) +
        geom_line(color = "#e74c3c", linewidth = 0.7) +
        geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
        labs(title = "Trend component", x = "Week index", y = "Trend (slope)") +
        theme_minimal(base_size = 11)
    ))
  }

  if (!all(is.na(comp$seasonal))) {
    plots <- c(plots, list(
      ggplot(comp, aes(index, seasonal)) +
        geom_line(color = "#2980b9", linewidth = 0.7) +
        geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
        labs(title = "Seasonal component (s1)", x = "Week index", y = "Seasonal") +
        theme_minimal(base_size = 11)
    ))
  }

  combined <- wrap_plots(plots, ncol = 1) +
    plot_annotation(
      title = paste0("Holt-Winters Components — ", label),
      theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13))
    )
  print(combined)
}
all_metrics <- list()
all_diag    <- list()
for (comm in commodities) {
  cat("\n", strrep("=", 60), "\n")
  cat("  Commodity:", comm, "\n")
  cat(strrep("=", 60), "\n")

  sub <- df %>% filter(commodity == comm) %>% arrange(date)

  if (!"split" %in% names(sub)) stop("'split' column not found in data.")

  train_df <- sub %>% filter(split == "train") %>% select(date, target_price) %>% drop_na(target_price)
  test_df  <- sub %>% filter(split == "test")  %>% select(date, target_price) %>% drop_na(target_price)

  if (nrow(train_df) < FREQ * 2 || nrow(test_df) == 0) {
    cat("  !! Insufficient rows — skipping.\n"); next
  }

  n_test   <- nrow(test_df)
  train_ts <- ts(train_df$target_price, frequency = FREQ)

  cat(sprintf("  Train: %s -> %s (%d weeks)\n",
              min(train_df$date), max(train_df$date), nrow(train_df)))
  cat(sprintf("  Test:  %s -> %s (%d weeks)\n",
              min(test_df$date),  max(test_df$date),  n_test))

  fc_list <- list()

  # ── Model A: HW Additive ──────────────────
  cat("\n  Fitting HW Additive (AAA)...\n")
  model_add <- tryCatch(
    ets(train_ts, model = "AAA", damped = FALSE),
    error = function(e) { cat("  !! HW Additive failed:", e$message, "\n"); NULL }
  )
  if (!is.null(model_add)) {
    fc_add   <- forecast(model_add, h = n_test)
    pred_add <- as.numeric(fc_add$mean)
    cat(sprintf("  alpha=%.4f  beta=%.4f  gamma=%.4f\n",
                model_add$par["alpha"], model_add$par["beta"], model_add$par["gamma"]))
    all_metrics[[paste(comm, "additive")]] <- compile_metrics(
      test_df$target_price, pred_add, train_df$target_price, model_add,
      paste0(comm, " — HW Additive")
    )
    all_diag[[paste(comm, "additive")]] <- residual_diagnostics(
      as.numeric(residuals(model_add)), model_add, paste0(comm, " — HW Additive")
    )
    fc_list[["Additive"]] <- fc_add
    plot_residuals(as.numeric(residuals(model_add)), as.numeric(fitted(model_add)),
                   paste0(comm, " — HW Additive"))
    plot_components(model_add, paste0(comm, " — HW Additive"))
  }

  # ── Model B: HW Multiplicative ────────────
  if (any(train_df$target_price <= 0, na.rm = TRUE)) {
    cat("  !! Non-positive values — skipping Multiplicative.\n")
    model_mult <- NULL
  } else {
    cat("  Fitting HW Multiplicative (MAM)...\n")
    model_mult <- tryCatch(
      ets(train_ts, model = "MAM", damped = FALSE),
      error = function(e) { cat("  !! HW Multiplicative failed:", e$message, "\n"); NULL }
    )
  }
  if (!is.null(model_mult)) {
    fc_mult   <- forecast(model_mult, h = n_test)
    pred_mult <- as.numeric(fc_mult$mean)
    cat(sprintf("  alpha=%.4f  beta=%.4f  gamma=%.4f\n",
                model_mult$par["alpha"], model_mult$par["beta"], model_mult$par["gamma"]))
    all_metrics[[paste(comm, "multiplicative")]] <- compile_metrics(
      test_df$target_price, pred_mult, train_df$target_price, model_mult,
      paste0(comm, " — HW Multiplicative")
    )
    all_diag[[paste(comm, "multiplicative")]] <- residual_diagnostics(
      as.numeric(residuals(model_mult)), model_mult, paste0(comm, " — HW Multiplicative")
    )
    fc_list[["Multiplicative"]] <- fc_mult
    plot_residuals(as.numeric(residuals(model_mult)), as.numeric(fitted(model_mult)),
                   paste0(comm, " — HW Multiplicative"))
    plot_components(model_mult, paste0(comm, " — HW Multiplicative"))
  }

  # ── Model C: ETS auto ─────────────────────
  cat("  Fitting ETS (auto ZZZ)...\n")
  model_ets <- tryCatch(
    ets(train_ts, model = "ZZZ"),
    error = function(e) { cat("  !! ETS auto failed:", e$message, "\n"); NULL }
  )
  if (!is.null(model_ets)) {
    fc_ets   <- forecast(model_ets, h = n_test)
    pred_ets <- as.numeric(fc_ets$mean)
    cat(sprintf("  ETS selected: %s\n", model_ets$method))
    cat(sprintf("  alpha=%.4f  beta=%s  gamma=%s\n",
                model_ets$par["alpha"],
                ifelse("beta"  %in% names(model_ets$par), round(model_ets$par["beta"],  4), "N/A"),
                ifelse("gamma" %in% names(model_ets$par), round(model_ets$par["gamma"], 4), "N/A")))
    all_metrics[[paste(comm, "ets_auto")]] <- compile_metrics(
      test_df$target_price, pred_ets, train_df$target_price, model_ets,
      paste0(comm, " — ETS (", model_ets$method, ")")
    )
    all_diag[[paste(comm, "ets_auto")]] <- residual_diagnostics(
      as.numeric(residuals(model_ets)), model_ets,
      paste0(comm, " — ETS (", model_ets$method, ")")
    )
    fc_list[["ETS (auto)"]] <- fc_ets
    plot_residuals(as.numeric(residuals(model_ets)), as.numeric(fitted(model_ets)),
                   paste0(comm, " — ETS (", model_ets$method, ")"))
    plot_components(model_ets, paste0(comm, " — ETS (", model_ets$method, ")"))
  }

  # ── Combined forecast plot ─────────────────
  if (length(fc_list) > 0) {
    plot_forecast(
      train_dates = train_df$date,
      train_vals  = train_df$target_price,
      test_dates  = test_df$date,
      test_vals   = test_df$target_price,
      fc_list     = fc_list,
      label       = comm
    )
  }
}
## 
##  ============================================================ 
##   Commodity: Beetroot 
## ============================================================ 
##   Train: 2016-01-22 -> 2024-12-20 (446 weeks)
##   Test:  2025-01-20 -> 2026-02-16 (28 weeks)
## 
##   Fitting HW Additive (AAA)...
##   !! HW Additive failed: Frequency too high 
##   Fitting HW Multiplicative (MAM)...
##   !! HW Multiplicative failed: Frequency too high 
##   Fitting ETS (auto ZZZ)...
## Warning in ets(train_ts, model = "ZZZ"): I can't handle data with frequency
## greater than 24. Seasonality will be ignored. Try stlf() if you need seasonal
## forecasts.
##   ETS selected: ETS(M,N,N)
##   alpha=0.8524  beta=N/A  gamma=N/A

## Don't know how to automatically pick scale for object of type <ts>. Defaulting
## to continuous.

metrics_df <- bind_rows(all_metrics)
diag_df    <- bind_rows(all_diag)

cat("\n\n", strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## 
## 
##  ======================================================================
cat("  HOLT-WINTERS MODEL PERFORMANCE METRICS\n")
##   HOLT-WINTERS MODEL PERFORMANCE METRICS
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## ======================================================================
print(metrics_df, n = Inf)
## # A tibble: 1 × 8
##   model                         RMSE    MAE  MASE sMAPE  MAPE   AIC   BIC
##   <chr>                        <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>
## 1 Beetroot — ETS (ETS(M,N,N)) 0.0806 0.0604  3.20  8.30  7.78 -551. -539.
cat("\n\n", strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## 
## 
##  ======================================================================
cat("  RESIDUAL DIAGNOSTICS\n")
##   RESIDUAL DIAGNOSTICS
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## ======================================================================
print(diag_df, n = Inf)
## # A tibble: 1 × 9
##   model   resid_mean resid_sd ljung_box_lag10_p ljung_box_lag20_p normality_test
##   <chr>        <dbl>    <dbl>             <dbl>             <dbl> <chr>         
## 1 Beetro…    0.00268   0.0498             0.258             0.547 Shapiro-Wilk  
## # ℹ 3 more variables: normality_p <dbl>, autocorr_ok <lgl>, normality_ok <lgl>
cat("\n\n", strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## 
## 
##  ======================================================================
cat("  BEST MODEL PER COMMODITY (by RMSE)\n")
##   BEST MODEL PER COMMODITY (by RMSE)
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## ======================================================================
metrics_df %>%
  mutate(commodity = str_extract(model, "^.+(?= —)")) %>%
  group_by(commodity) %>%
  slice_min(RMSE, n = 1) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  print(n = Inf)
## # A tibble: 1 × 9
##   model                      RMSE    MAE  MASE sMAPE  MAPE   AIC   BIC commodity
##   <chr>                     <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <chr>    
## 1 Beetroot — ETS (ETS(M,N… 0.0806 0.0604  3.20  8.30  7.78 -551. -539. Beetroot
cat("\n\n", strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## 
## 
##  ======================================================================
cat("  SMOOTHING PARAMETERS INTERPRETATION GUIDE\n")
##   SMOOTHING PARAMETERS INTERPRETATION GUIDE
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## ======================================================================
cat("
  alpha (level):    near 0 = slow adaptation | near 1 = weights recent obs heavily
  beta  (trend):    near 0 = trend changes slowly | near 1 = trend adapts quickly
  gamma (seasonal): near 0 = stable seasonal pattern | near 1 = updates aggressively\n\n")
## 
##   alpha (level):    near 0 = slow adaptation | near 1 = weights recent obs heavily
##   beta  (trend):    near 0 = trend changes slowly | near 1 = trend adapts quickly
##   gamma (seasonal): near 0 = stable seasonal pattern | near 1 = updates aggressively
write_csv(metrics_df, file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "hw_metrics_filtered.csv"))
write_csv(diag_df,    file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "hw_residual_diagnostics_filtered.csv"))

cat("✅ Done.\n")
## ✅ Done.
